message:
style(lib,indexer): replace assert!(false) with panic!, if-let-chain + test fmt

Pure clippy / rustfmt cleanup — no behaviour changes.

src/lib.rs
- subcommand_robot_output_tests: replace three `assert!(false, msg)
  + return;` pairs with direct `panic!(msg)`. Clippy flags
  `assert!(false, …)` as always-true-until-evaluated and suggests
  panic! for unreachable branches; the pattern here is "test
  precondition failed, bail", exactly what panic! is for.
- The last of the three blocks also tightens the let-else:
    `let Some(Commands::Search { mode, .. }) = cli.command.as_ref()
     else { assert!(matches!(command, Commands::Search {..})); return; };`
  collapses to:
    `let Commands::Search { mode, .. } = command else {
        panic!("expected search command, got {command:?}"); };`
  — same behaviour (panic when the test got back the wrong command
  shape), but uses `command` directly instead of the redundant
  re-match against `cli.command.as_ref()`.

src/indexer/mod.rs
- rustfmt rewrap of one long
  `fresh_storage.fallback_fts_is_known_healthy_for_archive_fingerprint(
   archive_fingerprint)?` chain inside an if-let-chain; the trailing
  dot now leads the wrapped segment. No logic change.

src/indexer/responsiveness.rs
- Test-module synthetic-load generator: collapse a nested
  `let load = if u < 0.95 { 0.2 + u * 0.3 } else { 0.9 + u * 0.2 };`
  form into rustfmt's preferred multi-line layout so the arithmetic
  reads top-to-bottom. Output values are bit-for-bit identical.
